parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ url: $('pick url').item.json.url, formats: ['markdown'], onlyMainContent: false, timeout: 100000 }) }}

options.systemMessage: You extract volleyball clubs from a national federation's club-directory page, given as Markdown (usually a table or list). Return ONLY a JSON object - no prose, no code fences - of shape {"clubs":[{"name":string,"city":string,"region":string,"website":string,"detail_url":string}]}. Include EVERY real club/society; ignore navigation, headers, filters, pagination and social links. For each club: name = the club's name (required; skip nameless rows); strip markdown bold (**) and any trailing category codes in parentheses. city = its town (strip postal codes and street address). region = state/county/regional federation if shown, else "". website = the club's OWN external website if shown (e.g. www.club.hr), else "" - NEVER a link that points back to this directory/listing or to the federation's own site. detail_url = THE HREF that this club's own name/row links to - its profile/detail page within this directory (e.g. /cluburi_volei/<slug>/ or ...?id=123). Copy the exact URL from the Markdown link; do NOT invent or guess; if the club has no such link, "". Use "" for any missing field, never null. If there are no clubs, return {"clubs":[]}.
